title: Baba Garib Sthan Mandir
tagline: Baba Garib Sthan Mandir — spiritual town of Bihar

overview.short: Garibnath Temple Muzaffarpur is a Hindu holy place in Muzaffarpur in the Indian state of Bihar.
overview.description: Garibnath Temple Muzaffarpur is a Hindu holy place in Muzaffarpur in the Indian state of Bihar. The temple is one of the oldest temples dedicated to Lord Shiva and known as the "Deoghar of Bihar" Devotees from all across the district and state come here to pray and wish for their better life ahead. It is believed that prayer during the month of Sawan has special significance, which helps to fulfill all wishes. The temple is very crowded during these days.

topPlaces.name: Muzaffarpur
topPlaces.description: Muzaffarpur is a city in the Muzaffarpur district, situated on the banks of the Burhi Gandak River in the Tirhut division of the Indian state of Bihar. It serves as the administrative headquarters of both the Tirhut division and the Muzaffarpur district.

topPlaces.name: Chaturbhuj Sthan
topPlaces.description: Chaturbhuj Sthan is a red-light district in Muzaffarpur, a district in the state of Bihar, India. The area is said to have existed since the Mughal period and is home to more than 3,500 sex workers.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Muzaffarpur
topPlaces.description: The Diocese of Muzaffarpur (Latin: Muzaffarpuren(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Muzaffarpur in the ecclesiastical province of Patna in India.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya Muzaffarpur
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya Muzaffarpur is a school in Muzaffarpur, Muzaffarpur district in Bihar, India. It was established on 31 October 1986.

topPlaces.name: Pragya Nagar
topPlaces.description: Pragya Nagar is a village in Muzaffarpur. Well-known teachers are residents.

topPlaces.name: North Bihar
topPlaces.description: North Bihar is the northern region of the Indian state of Bihar. It lies between the Ganga River in the south and the India–Nepal border in the north.

items.note: Muzaffarpur is a city in the Muzaffarpur district, situated on the banks of the Burhi Gandak River in the Tirhut division of the Indian state of Bihar. It serves as the administrative headquarters of both the Tirhut division and the Muzaffarpur district.
Chaturbhuj Sthan is a red-light district in Muzaffarpur, a district in the state of Bihar, India. The area is said to have existed since the Mughal period and is home to more than 3,500 sex workers.
The Diocese of Muzaffarpur (Latin: Muzaffarpuren(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Muzaffarpur in the ecclesiastical province of Patna in India.

Pragya Nagar is a village in Muzaffarpur. Well-known teachers are residents.
North Bihar is the northern region of the Indian state of Bihar. It lies between the Ganga River in the south and the India–Nepal border in the north.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baba Garib Sthan Mandir?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baba Garib Sthan Mandir is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baba Garib Sthan Mandir?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Muzaffarpur Junction (0 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baba Garib Sthan Mandir?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baba Garib Sthan Mandir at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baba Garib Sthan Mandir?
faq.a: Stays in Baba Garib Sthan Mandir start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baba Garib Sthan Mandir?
faq.a: The top attractions are Muzaffarpur, Chaturbhuj Sthan, Diocese of Muzaffarpur, Kendriya Vidyalaya Muzaffarpur, Pragya Nagar.
